---
title: "The Theory of Quantum Information"
subtitle: "John Watrous『The Theory of Quantum Information』学習ロードマップ"
page-layout: full
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
John Watrous『The Theory of Quantum Information』の内容を数学的に厳密、かつ視覚的に理解するための学習・解説リサーチ環境です。

:::

## 🗺️ ロードマップ (Textbook Roadmap)

| 項目名 | 原文 PDF | 教科書解説 |
| :--- | :--: | :--: |
| **Chapter 1: Mathematical Preliminaries** | [📄 p.1](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=9) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#chapter-1) |
| 　**1.1** Linear algebra | [📄 p.1](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=9) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#linear-algebra) |
| 　　**1.1.1** Complex Euclidean spaces | [📄 p.1](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=9) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#complex-euclidean-spaces) |
| 　　**1.1.2** Linear operators | [📄 p.8](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=16) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#linear-operators) |
| 　　**1.1.3** Operator decompositions and norms | [📄 p.24](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=32) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#operator-decompositions-and-norms) |
| 　**1.2** Analysis, convexity, and probability theory | [📄 p.35](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=43) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#analysis-convexity-probability) |
| 　　**1.2.1** Analysis and convexity | [📄 p.35](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=43) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#analysis-and-convexity) |
| 　　**1.2.2** Probability theory | [📄 p.47](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=55) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#probability-theory) |
| 　　**1.2.3** Semidefinite programming | [📄 p.53](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=61) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#semidefinite-programming) |
| | | |
| **Chapter 2: Basic notions of quantum information** | [📄 p.58](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=66) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#chapter-2) |
| 　**2.1** Registers and states | [📄 p.58](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=66) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#registers-and-states) |
| 　　**2.1.1** Registers and classical state sets | [📄 p.58](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=66) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#registers-and-classical-state-sets) |
| 　　**2.1.2** Quantum states of registers | [📄 p.61](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=69) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#quantum-states-of-registers) |
| 　　**2.1.3** Reductions and purifications of quantum states | [📄 p.67](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=75) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#reductions-and-purifications-of-quantum-states) |
| 　**2.2** Quantum channels | [📄 p.72](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=80) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#quantum-channels) |
| 　　**2.2.1** Definitions and basic notions | [📄 p.72](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=80) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#definitions-and-basic-notions) |
| 　　**2.2.2** Representations and characterizations | [📄 p.77](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=85) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#representations-and-characterizations) |
| 　　**2.2.3** Examples of channels | [📄 p.91](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=99) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#examples-of-channels) |
| 　　**2.2.4** Extremal channels | [📄 p.96](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=104) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#extremal-channels) |
| 　**2.3** Measurements | [📄 p.100](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=108) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#measurements) |
| 　　**2.3.1** Two equivalent definitions | [📄 p.100](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=108) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#two-equivalent-definitions) |
| 　　**2.3.2** Basic notions concerning measurements | [📄 p.105](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=113) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#basic-notions-concerning-measurements) |
| 　　**2.3.3** Extremal measurements and ensembles | [📄 p.113](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=121) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#extremal-measurements-and-ensembles) |
| | | |
| **Chapter 3: Similarity and distance among states and channels** | [📄 p.124](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=132) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#chapter-3) |
| 　**3.1** Quantum state discrimination | [📄 p.124](assets/Quantum_Information.pdf#page=132) | [📖 Textbook](textbook.qmd#quantum-state-discrimination) |

---

::: {.text-center}
*© 2025 Quantum Information Study Project*

:::
